summary: Iteration 20 - News Fetching Bug Fix Verification Complete. The celebrity news fetching is working correctly. Major celebrities (David Beckham, Taylor Swift, Katie Price) now return news articles. Hot Celebs banner displays celebrities with tier badges and prices. Frontend gracefully handles missing URLs with Google search fallback.

minor.issue: Some RSS feeds (Billboard, E! News, BuzzFeed) don't provide article URLs - frontend falls back to Google search

passed_tests: POST /api/celebrity/search - David Beckham returns celebrity data with news
POST /api/celebrity/search - Taylor Swift returns celebrity data with news (3 articles)
POST /api/celebrity/search - Katie Price returns celebrity data with news (5 articles)
News articles have required fields (title, source, date)
News articles have URLs (Katie Price: 5/5 with URLs)
GET /api/hot-celebs - Returns list of 18 hot celebrities
Hot celebs have required fields (name, tier, price)
Taylor Swift correctly identified as A-LIST tier
Frontend Hot Celebs banner displays correctly with tier badges
Frontend news panel shows on hover with clickable links
Frontend gracefully handles missing URLs with Google search fallback
critical_code_review_comments: News fetching algorithm improved with word-boundary matching and celebrity-specific aliases
fetch_real_celebrity_news function at line 1871 now correctly matches celebrities like 'beckham', 'swift'
Frontend CelebrityCard.jsx has good fallback for missing URLs (Google search)
Hot Celebs banner correctly displays tier badges (A-LIST, B-LIST, C-LIST, D-LIST) and prices
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_news_fetching.py - Created comprehensive test suite for news fetching
context_for_next_testing_agent: News fetching is working correctly. The fetch_real_celebrity_news function uses improved name matching with word boundaries and celebrity-specific aliases. Some RSS feeds (Billboard, E! News, BuzzFeed) don't provide direct article URLs, but the frontend handles this with a Google search fallback. Hot Celebs banner shows celebrities with tier badges and dynamic pricing based on news coverage.

taylor_swift.note: Billboard, E! News, BuzzFeed feeds don't provide URLs - frontend uses Google search fallback
